title: Capstone Scope, Dependency Graph, Milestones, and Risk Control · Claim Defense
summary: A review board compares near-miss decisions for capstone scope, dependency graph, milestones, and risk control and accepts only observable behavior, human evidence, explicit non-claims, and owned transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for evidence-ordered capstone delivery system. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: State the bounded product thesis, users, decision, outcomes, exclusions, quality floor, and credential claims in one falsifiable contract.
Order new tasks by prerequisite competence and schedule retrieval of earlier skills before their consequential use.
Create vertical milestones that retire a named risk, deliver an affected-user signal, and produce assessable evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Scope Thesis · changed case · Quiz Scope Thesis
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Component deadlines conceal prerequisite gaps, external waits, integration risk, missing evidence milestones, and no trigger for releasing scope. The quiz variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

Which action demonstrates Scope Thesis without dropping earlier contracts?

steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for state the bounded product thesis, users, decision, outcomes, exclusions, quality floor, and credential claims in one falsifiable contract. For Scope Thesis · changed case · Quiz Scope Thesis, keep Scope Thesis connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

options.text: A flexible broad vision is better than a bounded capstone thesis. That claim fails the scope thesis · changed case evidence boundary for Scope Thesis.

For scope thesis · changed case, apply Scope Thesis without its stated preconditions, omit the evidence-ordered capstone delivery system trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Scope Thesis for evidence-ordered capstone delivery system.

stimulus.title: Progress Evidence failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

Run the Progress Evidence boundary case for evidence-ordered capstone delivery system, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Progress Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Progress Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Component deadlines conceal prerequisite gaps, external waits, integration risk, missing evidence milestones, and no trigger for releasing scope. The quiz variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Evidence target: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Progress Evidence.

For Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Progress Evidence, keep Progress Evidence connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Track accepted evidence, changed assumptions, blocked claims, forecast, decision latency, quality debt, and residual risk rather than percent complete.
